conclusion.title: Upper Critical Fields and Coherence Lengths

conclusion: Based on electrical resistance measurements under magnetic fields up to 9 T, the authors derive the upper critical fields $H_{c2}(0)$ using the Werthamer–Helfand–Hohenberg (WHH) model: - $H_{c2}^{\mathrm{WHH}}(0\mathrm{K}) \approx 157$ T for $Im\text{-}3m$ $YH_{6}$. - $H_{c2}^{\mathrm{WHH}}(0\mathrm{K}) \approx 60$ T for $P6_3/mmc$ $YD_{9}$. - $H_{c2}^{\mathrm{WHH}}(0\mathrm{K})$ is estimated at 120 T for $YH_{9}$ (assuming similar Fermi velocities to $YD_9$). The corresponding Ginzburg-Landau coherence lengths $\xi(0\mathrm{K})$ are calculated to be 1.45–1.75 nm for $YH_{6}$ and 2.3–2.7 nm for $YD_{9}$.
